question: Will the highest temperature in Atlanta be between 90-91°F on March 22?
description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors 84-85°F (31.5% implied probability) for Atlanta's highest temperature on March 22, propelled by the latest GFS and ECMWF ensemble forecasts converging on peaks near 85°F amid a robust high-pressure ridge fostering southerly winds and adiabatic warming. Recent 12z model runs have boosted these odds over 82-83°F (26%), as boundary layer mixing enhances diurnal heating with low cloud cover probabilities below 20%. Key differentiators include soil moisture deficits from prior dry spells amplifying surface sensible heat flux for 84+°F, versus potential mid-afternoon cumulus onset capping at 83°F per SREF clusters (55% above 84°F). NOAA data confirms anomalously warm late-March patterns, with historical averages ~68°F.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward a Miami high temperature of 80-81°F (33% implied probability) or 82-83°F (27%), driven by the latest National Weather Service forecast predicting 81-83°F under high-pressure ridging and abundant sunshine, with minimal cloud interference. Ensemble models from GFS and ECMWF show a tight spread around 82°F at Miami International Airport, influenced by warm Atlantic sea surface temperatures near 78°F and urban heat island effects boosting afternoon peaks. Slightly cooler 78-79°F odds (19%) reflect potential sea-breeze moderation or isolated showers, per historical March norms averaging 79°F; divergence hinges on diurnal heating rates and convective timing, with model updates expected by evening.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a maximum temperature of 30°C or below in Lucknow on March 22 (66.5% implied probability), driven by the latest forecasts from the India Meteorological Department (IMD) projecting daytime highs around 28-30°C amid persistent cool northerly winds suppressing heat buildup. Recent observations show March 20-21 maxima at 29°C and 28°C, respectively, aligning with below-seasonal norms for late winter in the Gangetic plains, where average March peaks hover near 32°C. Minimal divergence in ensemble models from global centers like ECMWF reinforces this mild outlook, with low trader odds (under 3%) for 32°C+ reflecting negligible heatwave risks from current upper-air patterns. Upcoming hourly updates could nudge probabilities if cloud cover evolves.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Munich's March 22 high temperature clusters tightly around 12-14°C, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts implying a mean of about 13°C under a mild high-pressure ridge over Central Europe. This setup features southerly winds advecting warmer continental air, elevating odds above the late-March climatological average of 11-12°C observed at Munich Airport over the past three decades. Differentiating the frontrunners, 13°C leads due to model consensus on partial cloud breaks maximizing daytime insolation, while 12°C gains from potential overcast persistence per DWD short-range guidance, and 14°C hinges on fuller solar exposure in outlier drier scenarios—highlighting typical 1-2°C short-term forecast spread amid low synoptic volatility. Hourly updates from official stations will sharpen resolution.

question: Will the highest temperature in Munich be 8°C on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Munich Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Munich Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tilts toward a Tokyo high of 16°C on March 22, driven by the latest Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA) and ECMWF model ensembles projecting mild conditions under a building high-pressure ridge, with southerly winds advecting warmer air from the Pacific. This edges out 15°C (cooler northerly flow scenario) and 17°C (clearer skies boosting afternoon insolation), as historical March late averages hover around 14-15°C but urban heat island effects and above-normal sea surface temperatures add 1-2°C upside. Key differentiator: cloud cover uncertainty, with GFS runs split on partial overcast muting peaks below 16°C versus sunnier outcomes pushing 17°C; watch JMA's 12z update for resolution amid typical spring frontal variability.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tilts toward a Tokyo high of 16°C on March 22, driven by the latest Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA) and ECMWF model ensembles projecting mild conditions under a building high-pressure ridge, with southerly winds advecting warmer air from the Pacific. This edges out 15°C (cooler northerly flow scenario) and 17°C (clearer skies boosting afternoon insolation), as historical March late averages hover around 14-15°C but urban heat island effects and above-normal sea surface temperatures add 1-2°C upside. Key differentiator: cloud cover uncertainty, with GFS runs split on partial overcast muting peaks below 16°C versus sunnier outcomes pushing 17°C; watch JMA's 12z update for resolution amid typical spring frontal variability.

question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 17°C or below on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Hong Kong high of 24°C (42.5%) or 25°C (31.5%), driven by the Hong Kong Observatory's latest forecast predicting fine weather under a persistent high-pressure ridge, with afternoon temperatures peaking near 25°C on March 22. This aligns with recent observations—21 March hit 25.5°C amid sunny conditions and low cloud cover, elevating solar insolation and diurnal warming beyond March's typical 22°C median high. Ensemble weather models from ECMWF and GFS converge on 24-26°C, though minor trough influences could trim peaks by 1-2°C, explaining trailing odds for 26°C (15%) while sidelining cooler outcomes below 23°C. No tropical signals or fronts threaten disruptions.

Updated satellite observations and radiosonde data bolster confidence in these leading probabilities.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 18°C (27%) and 19°C (26.5%) as Madrid's highest temperature on March 22, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts converging on daytime highs of 18-19°C under a building high-pressure ridge advecting mild Atlantic air. AEMET's operational model aligns closely, projecting 18°C amid light southerly winds and partial cloudiness, while historical March 22 averages hover at 17°C. Differentiation stems from model spread: warmer outliers (20°C+) hinge on clearer skies and stronger subsidence, but ensemble means temper this to 18°C amid lingering stratocumulus decks; cooler 17°C odds reflect potential overnight cooling carryover. Traders eye tomorrow's 12Z updates for resolution shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors a Warsaw high of 14°C (39.5% implied probability), reflecting the latest ensemble forecasts from ECMWF and GFS models, which project peak daytime temperatures in the mid-teens Celsius amid a high-pressure ridge ushering mild Atlantic air into Central Europe. Recent observations from Poland's IMGW show March averages running 2-3°C above historical norms of 9-11°C, with yesterday's Warsaw maximum at 13°C supporting this warming trend. Lower odds for 13°C (21.5%) and 15°C+ (25.5%) account for model spread and diel variability, while slim chances below 12°C align with minimal cold air intrusion risks; watch afternoon updates for refinements ahead of March 22.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts slightly toward a Taipei high of 29°C or higher at 27.5% implied probability, driven by the Central Weather Administration's latest forecast indicating partly cloudy skies and highs reaching 28-30°C amid a high-pressure ridge suppressing cooler air masses. This edges out 26°C (24%) and 27-28°C outcomes (46% combined), reflecting ensemble model spread from ECMWF and GFS, where divergent cloud cover projections and urban heat island effects in Taipei could push peaks 1-2°C higher than seasonal March norms of 23-25°C. Recent soundings show low-level warmth advection, but a potential evening sea breeze introduces downside risk, keeping lower temps viable amid historical variability. Key watch: afternoon CWA update.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by Taipei's Central Weather Administration in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Taipei Central Weather Administration, specifically the highest reading under the "Temperature" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.cwa.gov.tw/V8/C/W/OBS_Station.html?ID=46692

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts slightly toward a Taipei high of 29°C or higher at 27.5% implied probability, driven by the Central Weather Administration's latest forecast indicating partly cloudy skies and highs reaching 28-30°C amid a high-pressure ridge suppressing cooler air masses. This edges out 26°C (24%) and 27-28°C outcomes (46% combined), reflecting ensemble model spread from ECMWF and GFS, where divergent cloud cover projections and urban heat island effects in Taipei could push peaks 1-2°C higher than seasonal March norms of 23-25°C. Recent soundings show low-level warmth advection, but a potential evening sea breeze introduces downside risk, keeping lower temps viable amid historical variability. Key watch: afternoon CWA update.

question: Will the highest temperature in Taipei be 28°C on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by Taipei's Central Weather Administration in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

question: Counter-Strike: MOUZ NXT vs Alpha Dominion Nation (BO1) - DraculaN Group A
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between MOUZ NXT and Alpha Dominion Nation in the DraculaN Group A, initially scheduled for March 29 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ NXT" if MOUZ NXT win the match against Alpha Dominion Nation.

This market will resolve to "Alpha Dominion Nation" if Alpha Dominion Nation win the match against MOUZ NXT.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: MOUZ NXT holds a decisive edge as the top seed in DraculaN Group A, driving trader consensus toward an 80% implied win probability in this high-variance BO1 against underdog Alpha Dominion Nation. The academy squad's recent dominance—3-0 sweeps in qualifiers and a flawless group stage run—stems from siuhy and Jimpphat's rifling prowess on maps like Mirage and Inferno, where they've posted 1.25+ HLTV ratings. Alpha Dominion struggles with inconsistency, dropping key clutches in losses to lower-tier foes, exacerbated by lineup flux and no head-to-head edge. Watch for map vetoes and momentum swings, as BO1 upsets spike 25% in tier-2 events, but NXT's depth and home-like server advantage solidify their favoritism.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Bitcoin ETF Flows on March 20?
description:
This market will resolve to "Positive" if total Bitcoin ETF flows on Friday, March 20, 2026 are greater than 0, and to "Negative" if they are less than 0.

If flows are exactly 0, the market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source is Farside Investors, specifically the ETF Flow tab available at https://farside.co.uk/btc/ in the "Total" column for the date specified in the title.

The total flows will be considered finalized for that day once flows for all ETF providers have been published. If data for any ETF provider remains unpublished by 12 PM ET, 2 days after the date specified in the title, the market will resolve based on all available data published up to that time.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable or fails to publish any data by 12 PM ET, 2 days after the specified date, the market will resolve 50-50.
